news.title: Oil higher, stocks steady as US-Iran peace talks approach
news.summary: Investors were cautious Wednesday ahead of a Federal Reserve rate decision and the start of US-Iran peace talks, with oil prices climbing and stocks holding steady.Oil tumbled in recent days as optimism grew there would be a lasting Middle East peace agreement, which would mean supplies would be back on track, but reversed course on Wednesday.

news.title: Tesla Defies Auto Industry Sales Slump, Goldman Raises Q2 Delivery Forecast
news.summary: Goldman Sachs expects rising Tesla sales at a time when the broader auto industry is struggling.  Goldman's forecast of 420,000 cars would be a 9.3% increase compared to Q2 2025, when Tesla delivered 384,122 vehicles.  Sales growth of that level would buck current trends for both Tesla — which has had two years of declining sales — and the overall auto industry — with sales projected to slip 2.6% year over year for the quarter, according to Cox Automotive.

news.title: SpaceX (SPCX) Stock Gets A 1.6 Million Contract Options Debut
news.summary: SpaceX, trading as NasdaqGS:SPCX, saw options on its stock launch within days of its IPO. Options trading on SPCX recorded over 1.6 million contracts on the first day. The debut ranked just behind Tesla and Nvidia for first day options volume. This early options listing is unusual for a newly public mega-cap company. news.title: Oil Rises 2% After Trump Says Iran Deal Not Final
news.summary: Oil prices rebounded after President Trump warned the U.S.-Iran interim peace deal set to be signed on Friday isn’t final and could still fall apart.

news.title: SpaceX’s Historic Post-IPO Rally Loses Steam as Retail Frenzy Cools
news.summary: The stunning rally in SpaceX shares is losing some steam.  The stock was trading 6% lower this morning, after a staggering rally that propelled shares nearly 50% above its IPO price in less than three full days of trading.  It may be that a frenetic wave of demand from individual investors is finally fizzling out.

news.title: China’s Brutal Auto Price War Claims Its Biggest Victim Yet: BMW
news.summary: BMW  stock fell on Wednesday, dragging down shares of other European auto makers after a shocking cut to guidance that boils down to a weakening Chinese auto market.  Shares of the German car maker were down 7.8% in overseas trading.  The drops came after BMW cut its 2026 automotive profit margin guidance to 1% to 3%, down from 4% to 6%.
